steps.title: Mark the culmination
steps.content: The passage describes Conyers and Chisholm resubmitting the bill every session for fifteen years. The final sentence reports the long-awaited result in 1983. The transition should show that the bill passed after sustained effort.

steps.title: Reject nonculminating transitions
steps.content: Choice A signals contrast instead of eventual success. Choice B signals similarity, which does not fit the passage's timeline. Choice D treats the passage as adding another point rather than reaching a result. The answer is C.
